content.problemStatement:
Chef knows the projected sales of his store for the next NNN days. The projected sales of the ithi^{th}ith day is AiA_iAi‚Äč.

On any particular day, Chef can announce that his shop is closing which will **double** his sales for that day, but since Chef is a man of his word, he won't make any sale after that day.

Find the **maximum** possible total sales which Chef can make.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN  ‚ÄĒ the number of days.
The second line consists of NNN space-separated integers denoting the projected sales of each day.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** total sales that Chef can make.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef announces the closing on second day. Chef can double the sales of the second day to get 1+2‚čÖ2=51 + 2\cdot2 = 51+2‚čÖ2=5 total sales.
Test case 222: Chef announces the closing on first day. Chef can double the first day sales but will have no sale on the second and third day. This gives Chef a total sales of 7‚čÖ2=147\cdot2 = 147‚čÖ2=14.
